prompt_used: Create original premium horror artwork for a commercial alternate reality investigation game. Subject: villain portrait. Story world: The Fourth Tenant. Premise: Rent is collected from an apartment that appears on no official map.. Setting: Flood-district prewar apartment block. Threat presence: The Landlord of Rooms That Move. Recurring motifs: door chains, wet plaster, rent slips. Design portrait of antagonist The Landlord of Rooms That Move with archetype INTIMATE_WHISPERER, speech style "Close, familiar, and unnervingly domestic.", and motifs door chains, wet plaster, rent slips.. Subgenre: supernatural mystery. Tone: EERIE. Immediate scene brief: The Fourth Tenant - Villain Portrait. Grounded cinematic horror, photoreal detail, rich atmosphere, practical lighting, believable materials, analog grain, subtle lens bloom, visible depth. No title treatment, no UI overlay, no case notes panel, no labels, no watermark, no logo, no poster typography, no debug text. Create a realistic portrait with wardrobe, posture, facial tension, and environmental context that reveal personality and danger. Avoid floating-head composition. Frame as a prestige horror still, not concept art.
source_prompt: Story: The Fourth Tenant (the-fourth-tenant) | Hook: Rent is collected from an apartment that appears on no official map. | Subgenre: supernatural mystery | Tone: EERIE | Location: Flood-district prewar apartment block | Villain: The Landlord of Rooms That Move | Villain worldview: People accept impossible rooms when paperwork feels safe. | Motifs: door chains, wet plaster, rent slips.
